Ist eigentlich n cooles Spiel. 5 Figuren spielen gegeneinander man kann also eigentlich Rasenschach nennen. Ich bin natรผrlich der Abwehrchef, ja manchmal knall ich voll am Ball vorbei aber wer nicht wagt der nicht gewinnt. Ich mache keiner Fehler. Jedoch wird es problematisch wenn man die Niete Rico im Tor hat. Drauรen aufm Feld ist der wirklich n Flรผgelflitzer und tanzt die Gegner aus, sofern nicht ein Gegner einfach gegen sein Drippling aka ZikZak laufen ankommt. Dann gibt es noch die Mistmade Kilian welcher einfach denkt er ist Neymar Jr Prime und drippelt in den Favelas rum, nur dann reinzukacken. Von Keano brauch ich eigentlich gar nicht erst anfangen. Der ist erste Halbzeit AFK und zweite Halbzeit steht der im Tor. Der wird eigentlich immer ins Tor geschickt, wie damals immer aufm Bolzplatz. Und wenn er mal nicht im Tor steht schreit der Bande und schieรt den Ball einmal von Kanto direkt nach New York. Der einzige der halbwegs Kicken kann ist Potter, aber der sucht manchmal eher den Goldenen Schnatz anstelle des gegnerischen Tors. Ich frag mich halt nach welchen Ideen spielt er. Er kommt ins Training und macht nur was er will. Es ist kein einziger Ball dabei fรผr unser Spiel, es ist nur sein eigenes Spiel. Hier noch n Trick da noch n Trick und hier noch ne Idee und keine einzige klappt davon.

The game is fun to play, rewarding, a game like this was much needed.

The devs are listening to the players, but I feel that they don't implement any, even simple changes, which even somewhat relate to cosmetics, and would really positively impact lower end PC players, such as me. 

The more complex [b]AR[/b]'s lower my [b]FPS[/b] by about 10, which is about a third of my max FPS in-match.
The randomly chosen [b]opponent jerseys[/b] also can really mess with me, as I have a basic laptop, with sometimes not enough brightness even, not the best colors, and I can't differentiate between the two teams' players.

These are things that should be in my control. What AR's I see [i](if they impact vision or performance)[/i], and what jerseys my opponents wear [i](if they impact vision)[/i], but somehow these simple fixes don't get implemented, even though some bigger suggesstions by the community do.

Rematch has a fun concept, but the current state of the game is frustrating.

3v3 is basically keep-away simulator. As soon as a team gets a lead, they can spend the last couple of minutes passing the ball around with very little risk. Instead of encouraging attacking play, the game rewards wasting time and running out the clock.

The kickoff system makes this even worse. The team that gets scored on receives possession after a goal, which is fine in theory. The problem is that if you're still trailing by a goal and finally score late in the match, possession goes right back to the other team. In a game with only 4-minute matches, that often means they can immediately start playing keep-away and burn out the remaining time. It feels like the game punishes comebacks instead of creating exciting finishes.

The servers don't help either. My ping is consistently higher than it is in other online games, and the lag makes an already frustrating experience even worse. Missed touches, delayed reactions, and inconsistent connections can decide matches more than skill.

There's a good game in here somewhere, but right now the combination of a stale 3v3 meta, questionable possession rules, and poor server performance makes it hard to recommend.

Frustrant, c'est le mot qui qualifie le mieux ce jeu.

Entre les joueurs qui se prennent pour Messi, mais perdent la balle au premier dรฉfenseur arrivรฉ, Les Gardiens qui refusent obstinรฉment de rester aux cages et qui concรจdent des buts bรชtement โฆ

Les matchs en 3vs3, ou lorsque l'on est joueur solo, notre rรดle ce limite ร  รชtre spectateurโฆ ne recevant jamais de ballon.

Pourtant lorsque par un miracle, nous tombons sur de bons joueurs, mettant le collectif avant l'individualisme, le jeu est vraiment amusant, les enchainements de passes, la construction du jeu, sont vraiment agrรฉable !

En soit le jeu est bien, mais le plaisir qu'on en tire dรฉpend รฉnormรฉment des joueurs avec lesquels le matchmaking nous met.

N'ayant que le choix entre recommandรฉ ou non, je dirai oui, de prรฉfรฉrence avec des amis.

As someone who typically doesn't enjoy online competitive gameplay, it turns out I ADORE online cooperative team based fighting games loosely related to the idea of soccer. This is closer to rocket league on legs than it is to the world cup, but man getting into promotionals on online ranked with your friends really makes your blood race. When the pressure is on i can really feel the adrenaline, i curse my own shot selection when I make an nonoptimal choice, I verbally celebrate when a well place pass becomes  a scoring assist. The comradery my group and I feel when we're in the moment is among the best feelings in gaming right now for me. Rematch rocks.

This game honestly disappointed me

i used to play this on early july 2025 on gamepass but then no longer touch it, i already was having some inconveniences in terms of competitiveness but i didnt really care since i only used to play for fun and with friends

i came back, bought it and i only lasted 10 minutes without wanting to kms, i dont know if its because im washed but the movement and overall any mechanic is way โฅโฅโฅโฅโฅโฅโฅ now, movement is โฅโฅโฅโฅโฅโฅโฅ, i cant even move directions by 3 degrees because i will get momentum punished, the gameplay is way more luck based than skill based, the balance is a myth in this game, if FIFA was already known for "scripted games" this game is straight up a โฅโฅโฅโฅโฅโฅโฅ theater, touches or shots will only happen whenever the โฅโฅโฅโฅโฅโฅโฅ game wants to

the game is meant to be "automatic" but if so then this is a fcking 2000's automatic transmission, sht is so unreliable, you're not even sure if your move is going to apply properly like the dive doing random โฅโฅโฅโฅ, the control not even controlling or the character becoming mayweather prime to dodge a cross that was already 9 centuries in the air

dribbling is now so โฅโฅโฅโฅโฅโฅโฅ stiff it gets me to think about life, like genuinely someone who doesnt practice sports has better movement than this
back then the CTRL mode was supposed to be the close control dribble and it was the solution to stiff movement but not even that โฅโฅโฅโฅ survived, its laggy as โฅโฅโฅโฅ and doesnt even care about what move i did because it resets and keeps me on the same momentum

the game was good, only if it didnt โฅโฅโฅโฅโฅโฅโฅ do whatever it wants when you try to perform anything

and i dont even wanna talk about the players, straight bots, thats the reason i quit in the first place, one mistake or one "Good Job" and they all start tweaking and they never get punished, but if i lose connection for 5 seconds i do, are we deadass

The game hasnt changed much since then, it just got terribly worse, its too dry, all it got new is that theres new accessories, like son, fix your โฅโฅโฅโฅ first, there are bugs that since release [and beta] are still to this day as if it was a chronic disease

i bought it again because it was on discount but not even with the discount its worth it, honeslty, i dont recommend this to anyone unless you got friends to troll with for 20 mins

dont buy this, if you wanna play a real football-styled game, atp i just recommend playing irl, this market wont drop a good game yet and doesnt seem to do soon, i guarantee you anything else will be more fun than these casino ass mechanics

[h3] Should you buy Rematch? If you're asking that, the answer is yes.
 [/h3]

This game magically captures the spirit of fรบtbol.

I played in Season0 (on gamepass for shame!) and I primarily play ranked. Let's just say that I have a special jersey on that account. 

The game is way better now than launch, objectively speaking there are less bugs, less exploits, stronger connections, and better net sync.

The matchmaking is pretty solid but it's never perfect. 

[b] You are going to lose games. [/b] Ideally around 50% of them by default. 

Highly recommend it for people who have the fortitude for online PVP. But that isn't to say that the community is toxic. I've made plenty of new friends playing this game. Sure there will always be people with short tempers and little patience, but honestly the games are only 5minutes long so you only have to deal with a troublesome person for a little bit. And you can always mute everyone and emaotes/callouts.

Be light and bring your best, we'll see you out there.

So far so good, really fun game.
Seems way harder than it looks. Controls are simple, skill ceiling is EXTREMELY high.
It is a refreshment from games like EAFC (previously FIFA), PES or any of these typical mainstream football games. 

Lobbies are quick from my experience, though I am from Europe so I see people sometimes have over 100 ping though it does not bother me that much. The only problems I have with my teammates sometimes they are toxic with the in-game voice lines spamming "Good Job!" when you concede a goal or do a bad play. Other one is that they are really selfish and do not pass the ball when there are clear opportunities and free goals if they just become a team player.

I have had this game for 2 days so far, played about 9-10 hours total but it seems really promising, so if you think about buying it, just do it. It is really refreshing.

They should rename this game to "Ronaldo Simulator", since that's essentially all it is. As soon as your "teammates" get a hold of the ball, it's over. They'll run mindlessly towards the goal, shooting straight at it, no matter the distance. No passing, nothing. It doesn't matter if the entire opposing team is posted up directly in front of them in a straight horizontal line and you're standing there completely unguarded with the nearest McDonald's being closer than the opposing teams defenders, they'll rather just whack the ball directly at them, than passing it to someone standing even just an inch lower than them. Then, when the opposing team manages to obtain the ball, NOBODY will bother defending ANYTHING and they will scream at you in voice chat and spam quick-chat, if you, as a goalie, DARE to loose against the entire teams field players storming towards you all at once, with nobody in their way to defend, since all of them wasted their entire stamina spamming shots directly at the goalie without a single thought beforehand.

This game is amazing and a ton of fun. There are a lot of technical aspects from playing actual soccer that translate well onto here when it comes to dribbling and shooting that is difficult to replicate in other soccer games. 

The only downside is the egotistical sense of self that motivates humans. Playing this game with randoms can be DREADFUL as these narcists love to hog the ball and end up making a fool of themselves and costing the team the game. If you have at least 2 friends to play with, definitely get this game.
So far more fun and less enraging moments than FC 2026, nice :)

Um dos maiores problemas de Rematch รฉ como o lag pode acabar oferecendo vantagem em diversas situaรงรตes. Em vez de a partida ser decidida apenas pela habilidade dos jogadores, muitas vezes ela รฉ influenciada por problemas de sincronizaรงรฃo entre o que acontece na tela e o que o servidor registra. Isso gera situaรงรตes frustrantes, como roubar a bola e vรช-la voltar para o adversรกrio, fazer uma defesa clara com o goleiro e ainda assim sofrer o gol segundos depois, carrinhos que atravessam jogadores sem qualquer lรณgica aparente ou passes que parecem perfeitos na sua tela, mas sรฃo interpretados de outra forma pelo servidor. Na prรกtica, jogadores com conexรฃo instรกvel podem se tornar imprevisรญveis para os demais, comprometendo a integridade competitiva da partida.

Outro sinal de que o jogo foi lanรงado sem o polimento necessรกrio sรฃo os exploits descobertos pela prรณpria comunidade. Um dos exemplos mais absurdos foi quando os jogadores perceberam que pular era mais rรกpido do que correr, transformando as partidas em uma cena bizarra de jogadores atravessando o campo no pulo, parecendo golfinhos. A repercussรฃo foi tรฃo negativa que a desenvolvedora precisou agir para corrigir o problema. Situaรงรตes como essa demonstram falhas no balanceamento do jogo, pois quando uma mecรขnica quebrada oferece uma vantagem clara, o objetivo deixa de ser jogar futebol da melhor forma possรญvel e passa a ser explorar brechas do sistema para vencer. Isso enfraquece a experiรชncia competitiva e prejudica quem busca um jogo baseado em habilidade e estratรฉgia.

Un gran juego, muy disfrutable, sobre todo al jugarlo con amigos, debido a que la gente nunca pasa el balรณn en partida. Hay veces que el jugador no agarra el balรณn cuando deberรญa y estarรญa bien que los regates exitosos contasen para la puntuaciรณn final de la partida.

A combination of Janky controls, horrible customization options, and the worst PC controls make this a controller exclusive circus. I love when Ronoldo_Meatrider37 refuses to pass the ball and instead fails his rainbow flick for the 8th time. The hardest part about this game isn't the horrible controls, serverside lag, or imput lag while playing on a 5G hardwired PC; but instead the hardest part is trying to make a half decent looking character that doesn't look like a grotesque eldritch monstrosity with the horrible lack of customization and even worse customization options. Like we added vitiligo before heterochromia? who asked for any of this.

Honestly one of my least favorite game releases in recent years. Me and so many of my friends were excited about this game during the open beta, but the developers absolutely killed all of our excitement as the game went on. Spending the first few months focusing on updating the item shop, the battlepass, and gameplay changes instead of enabling crossplay as they had promised at release. By the time crossplay was finally implemented the vast majority of my friends had quit the game as there was no real competitive matchmaking (it's just completely random queuing and MMR isn't taken into account), and so many of the quality of life features that so many had been screaming for such as clubs had been completely ignored. This in my opinion makes competitive achievements mean nothing, is being Elite truly an achievement when half the time you will end up queuing against bronzes and silvers? Honestly in my opinion the gameplay has just regressed from the beta, and the development path that the studio decided to take truly makes me feel like this game was built to simply be a money grab rather then a long term game to enjoy which is truly disappointing given how good this game was during the beta. After convincing multiple of my friends to purchase the game I not only feel like I got scammed, but so did several of my friends so if you're considering getting this game, do yourself a favor and just play something else instead.

O que dizer sobre Rematch? Esse jogo traz um futebol rรกpido, colorido e, por vezes, bem divertido.

No entanto, para aqueles que carregam uma longa trajetรณria no EA Sports FC (e/ou saudoso FIFA) e eFootball (e/ou saudoso PES), esse jogo pode parecer complicado inicialmente porque demanda comandos diferentes, e os tradicionais nรฃo se aplicam totalmente.

Quando vocรช se acostuma, o jogo pode ficar bem legal para vocรช, especialmente nos modos nรฃo ranqueados, porque vocรช tem um jogo mais casual, livre e passรญvel de contribuir para sua familiarizaรงรฃo com o jogo.

Obtive algumas horas de jogo para poder avaliar adequadamente e posso dizer que [b]o jogo รฉ divertido para jogar casualmente[/b], fรกcil de obter grande parte das conquistas na Steam, tem um passe de batalha bacana e apresenta umas boas collabs. Porรฉm, vocรช pode acabar se irritando com trรชs coisas que, muitas vezes, atrapalharam minha jogabilidade: jogadores saindo da equipe e/ou fazendo gol contra porque nรฃo gostaram de algo (o jogo precisa de um sistema mais rรญgido de puniรงรตes); a mecรขnica quase imbatรญvel de tabelar a bola na parede ou bater embaixadinha atรฉ acontecer uma bicicleta imparรกvel; e, por fim, os dribles infinitos sem stamina.

Portanto, eu aconselho vocรช a ter paciรชncia, ainda mais que vocรช nรฃo vai lidar com o bendito handicap e a loteria de cartinhas. O jogo fica perfeito jogando com amigos porque tem muitos jogadores que se acham protagonistas e monopolizam a posse de bola. Ademais, eu gostaria de ressaltar que gostei do passe de batalha porque vocรช pode recuperar as moedas gastas, mas isso demanda um grind absurdo para cumprir missรตes diรกrias e semanais (o que vale a pena para quem gosta muito do jogo), e as recompensas imediatas nรฃo costumam ser muito boas. Tambรฉm acho importante ressaltar que o jogo estรก apenas em sua quarta temporada. Entรฃo, sua margem de evoluรงรฃo ainda รฉ bem alta.

I used to love playing this game when it first launched a year ago. After a long break, I recently jumped back into a 3vs3 ranked match, and unfortunately, I encountered a major flaw that completely ruins the competitive experience.

During the match, a player on our own team took the ball and just stood near our own goal line for almost the entire 5 minutes. The opponents couldn't even try to tackle him because doing so would risk leaving their own net wide open and conceding a goal. As a result, everyone was forced to just stand around and watch the clock run down in the most boring way possible.

The developers urgently need to implement an anti-griefing mechanic to prevent this kind of toxic behavior. Until this game-breaking issue is fixed, I cannot recommend this game. It's simply unplayable.

Avevo i miei dubbi, lo ammetto. Un gioco di calcio in terza persona dove controlli un solo giocatore in campo. fatto da quelli di [i]Sifu[/i]? Sulla carta poteva andare in qualsiasi direzione. Poi l'ho aperto con gli amici e ho capito subito che era stupendo.
Perchรฉ [i]Rematch[/i] da solo ha senso, ma รจ in gruppo che esplode. ร lรฌ il cuore di tutto. Ti ritrovi in tre, quattro a urlarvi addosso, a cercare lo smarcamento, a sbagliare il passaggio piรน facile del mondo e a ridere come scemi quando uno fa autogol perchรฉ ha premuto il tasto sbagliato. Ed รจ proprio questo il bello all'inizio: sei totalmente incapace. Ricordarsi i comandi รจ un'impresa, il pallone fa quello che vuole, le prime partite sono un disastro completo. Ma รจ un disastro divertente.
E poi succede la cosa che ti tiene incollato: cresci. Senza accorgertene cominci a capire i tempi, gli smarcamenti, i tiri vengono come li avevi in testa. La soddisfazione di azzeccare una giocata che due ore prima ti sembrava impossibile รจ quella roba lรฌ che ti fa dire "ancora una partita". Si sente l'esperienza di Sloclap nel feeling dei comandi: tutto risponde bene, ogni movimento ha il suo peso.
Non รจ perfetto, qualche problema di connessione e desync ogni tanto si fa sentire. Ma รจ una di quelle cose che con gli amici diventa quasi secondaria, perchรฉ il divertimento c'รจ ed รจ tanto. Da soli scendete di un punto buono, quindi il consiglio รจ semplice: trovate quattro amici e buttatevici.

[h1]Pro[/h1]
+ In gruppo รจ puro divertimento, qui dร  il meglio di sรฉ
+ Curva di crescita appagante: vedere quanto migliori รจ una soddisfazione continua
+ Feeling dei comandi ottimo, si sente la mano di Sloclap
+ Caotico e ridicolo all'inizio, nel senso piรน bello del termine

[h1]Contro[/h1]
- Qualche problema di connessione e desync
- Da soli perde parecchio del suo senso
- La curva di apprendimento iniziale puรฒ scoraggiare chi cerca soddisfazioni immediate

[h1]Voto[/h1]
โโโโโโโโโฐโฐ
8/10

[i](Il voto si basa su impressioni mie personali complessive probabilmente anche date dal momento personale in cui lo gioco, prendendo in giudizio solo questo gioco, senza confrontarlo con altri giochi.)[/i]

Really disappointing.

I tried to get back into this game after taking a very long break, and the same issues are still there from launch: horrible netcode, abysmal matchmaking, random disconnects, and endless bugs. But don't worry, the store tab is always updated and seems to work great!

I can also say that, as a solo player, this game can be incredibly miserable to play. More so than any other multiplayer game I've ever played, premades in this game typically treat you like you don't exist. I usually wind up playing pretty defensively, since it's the only time you really ever get a chance at interacting with the ball.

If you have friends to play it with and don't take it too seriously, I'm sure this game is fun. But if you are competitive or plan to play alone, I can't recommend this game in its current state.

I really wanted to enjoy REMATCH, but with every update the game seems to get worse instead of better.

The servers feel like they're literally made of potatoes. A few weeks ago I was getting around 40โ50 ms on European servers, which was perfectly playable. Now I'm constantly sitting at 120+ ms for no apparent reason. The increased latency makes the game feel sluggish and unresponsive, especially in crucial moments where every second matters.

On top of that, the game is plagued with bugs and glitches that completely ruin matches. Players get stuck, animations break, the ball behaves unpredictably, and desync issues are becoming more common with every patch. Instead of fixing existing problems, it feels like each update introduces a whole new set of issues.

What's most frustrating is that the core gameplay is actually fun, but the technical state of the game is dragging it down. The developers need to focus less on adding new content and more on fixing the servers, improving optimization, and addressing the growing list of bugs.

At this point, REMATCH feels like a game with great potential that is being held back by poor server performance and a lack of quality control. Unless major improvements are made, it's difficult to recommend this game to anyone.

I was really excited to finally buy this game. I even sold one of my Steam items to get it, so I had been looking forward to playing it for quite a while. Unfortunately, it didn't live up to my expectations. I've spent a decent amount of time in both Pro Soccer Online and Rocket Leagueโthe two games this is most comparable toโand despite that, Rematch failed to hook me during my first two hours with it.

I'm returning this within the 2-hour limit because I didn't enjoy it. Even at half price ($7.49 USD), I don't think it's worth it.

On the gameplay side, there's too much going on for a competitive game. The animations feel excessive, and there's a strange slow-mo effect when you shoot that only you can see, which breaks the pace. It also takes time to learn the mechanics, and 2 hours simply isn't enough to get there.

On the value side, I could've put that money toward 2 or 3 other games instead, especially since a 10-15 minute intro ate into my 2-hour return window before I'd even started playing properly. Matchmaking didn't help either: my opponents seemed to know the game decently, while my own team was full of newbies, AFK players, or people who clearly didn't know what they were doing. Steam also claims it's more enjoyable with a controller, and while I'm not a controller person for online competitive games, a game shouldn't need a specific input method to feel playable.

I've put a decent amount of time into both Pro Soccer Online and Rocket League, and I'd rather play Rocket League. PSO lost a lot of its player base after switching to Unreal Engine, but Rocket League still holds up. Seemed like developers wanted to make an Tsubasa game and couldn't. My suggestion: skip this one.

It's a solid game with a high skill ceiling. Unfortunately I can't really recommend it in it's current state. Every exploit gets abused to the maximum. Some exploits were annoying, and eventually got fixed. But everytime one gets patched another pops up or remains. The current "feign/fake shot" exploit is cancerous for this game, it could probably kill the remaining playerbase, given the game is already not doing great, only really being held up by die-hards and gamepass members.

The exploit is dumb easy, so if you play right now you will see it in every match and if you are just learning to play you'll probably just close the game and never play it again. All you have to do is hit Esc (Start/Menu button) during any actions with a wind-up, perfect feign, easy no skill wins in lower ranks and QP... for the losers who are helping kill the game they love so much.

Edit: Fake shot exploit was patched, and implemented as a mechanic in Season 4. This is a welcome change, but still isn't enough to get me to recommend it yet. Fun game with lots of problems, not all of which are even the fault of the devs.

Jogaรงo pรด, รฉ rocket league sรณ que ainda mais simples, divertido demais.

The Good

- Playing with friends is genuinely fun and creates some great moments that are difficult to find in traditional sports games.

- The gameplay is much more interactive and team-oriented than most arcade soccer titles, making individual plays feel rewarding when they come together.

- Character customization is surprisingly fleshed out. Appearance, voice, body type, and other cosmetic options give players a lot of freedom to create their own identity.

- Custom kits are a great feature and help teams feel unique rather than looking like clones of one another.

- Every player has the opportunity to play every position on the field, which encourages versatility and prevents players from locking themselves into a single role every match.

- Easy to learn, difficult to master. The core gameplay loop is accessible for new players while still rewarding skill development.

The Bad

- The netcode is simply not good enough for a game that relies on precise movement and split-second reactions. Lag, desync, and inconsistent connections regularly decide plays.

- Server performance is inconsistent even when playing with friends on the same regional servers, making matches feel unreliable. 

- Solo queue is miserable. The game heavily depends on teamwork, yet many players refuse to pass, play selfishly, or actively sabotage team play. Asking random teammates to cooperate was often met with slurs instead of communication.

- A recent update introduced a major new mechanic (shot cancelling) that significantly altered the skill ceiling after full release. Changes of that magnitude should have been tested publicly or discussed with the player base beforehand.

- Goalkeeping feels inconsistent and unpredictable. Success often depends more on preemptive guessing due to the netcode than reaction-based play. A player on >60 ping will be able to glitch his way past your defense, and then it is basically a roll of the dice if you can save it or not.

- Goalkeeper animations frequently fail to match the actual outcome. I've had multiple situations where I visibly caught the ball on my screen, only for the game to register a goal anyway. Also there is a fated "slow dive" that is uncontrollable.

- Desync between what players see and what the server registers creates frustrating moments that make it difficult to trust the game's competitive integrity.

- The game's biggest problems are technical rather than mechanical. Losing because you got outplayed is fine; losing because the game disagrees with what happened on your screen is not.


I paid ~$25 for REMATCH and got 100+ hours out of it. There is absolutely a fun game here, and I wouldn't have put that much time into it if there wasn't. Unfortunately, the issues above consistently outweigh the fun for me,  and I cannot recommend it to anyone.

EDIT - Through means of technowizardry (watching a load of youtube videos about optimising windows for SSDs, which did... nothing) I discovered three things I hadn't known when making this review. 1. windows + r - %temp% and delete can help for a few games, doesn't fix it though. 2. I was running the wrong .exe as admin, the one you need to find is in Steam\steamapps\common\Rematch\Runtime\Binaries\Win64 3. and the only thing I have discovered to work: low graphics settings, ultra anti-aliasing. My GPU has no problems running the game at higher settings, but for some reason it stops the 100% locked disk usage issue that freezes your PC. Since applying both 2 and 3 I have played another 12 hours, closing and reopening the game multiple times throughout the day to play more and have had no issues. Idk if this fixes it for other people but it still doesn't change the gist of my review. Fun game, scratches the rocket league itch, technical nightmare. If you don't have hours and hours of time to adjust every setting under the sun to get it to work and just stumble upon something that works, or you just value your time more than me then don't buy this game. I didn't start having problems until well past the refund period so I wouldn't risk it.

Bought this game the other day, had a really fun first night on it, despite the fact I was very bad, but could feel myself starting to get the hang of it. Go on it today, and all of a sudden I'm getting booted out of games (and punished with a leaver's penalty, meaning I can't queue another match for a few minutes) fine, I think to myself: "internet issue?" no. All of a sudden my PC becomes completely unresponsive. I go to task manager. Disk usage locked at 100%!!

Have checked all the supposed "fixes". At best I get 1 or 2 games before the same thing starts to happen again. People have been posting about this same issue for a YEAR. A FULL YEAR. How hard is it to resolve such issues before you charge people money for a game that ultimately proves to be non-functional?? I'd say the devs should be ashamed of themselves, but with the amount of cosmetic brand and player collaborations they've released in place of making sure their game works, their capacity to feel shame is clearly non-existent. 

It's a shame too. With Rocket League being Epic exclusive this is the kind of game that scratches that itch for me and doubtlessly has the capacity to for others, and even the free options for customisation are cool, but none of that matters when the game you've bought simply doesn't work. I'd refund this game had the bug started occurring in the refund period. Frankly, I'll still try, because I feel like I was sold a promising idea and received a broken mess in its place. Thank god it was on sale. Wouldn't recommend until the devs get their โฅโฅโฅโฅ together and get stuff which is so simple yet so gamebreaking fixed. And it's not my hardware, no other game does this, and any that have in the past *cough cough FIFA 23 cough cough* there has been a fix that has worked for me.

reviews.review: Not only does this World Cup have absolutely nothing to do with the real thing, but the selection of teams is also as ridiculous as it gets. Not to mention that they added that โฅโฅโฅโฅโฅโฅโฅ russian rag

The game is fun to play *with friends*, but there are pretty major technical issues that have existed since beta.
I think there are enough reviews making their case about these technical issues, I would like to add something else:

What really bugs me is the matchmaking and the social aspect of the game. When I report one of the *many* griefers and toxic players in this game, why do I get queued up with them again next match? I don't expect them to get punished just because I flag them, but why am I not automatically avoiding that player in the matchmaking pool for some time?
It's not the biggest issue, because the solution is to just wait a minute before queueing again. I just thought it was very odd for this feature to not be in the matchmaking system already.
And why can't I mute a toxic player's callouts? Having to hear "good job" spam from someone refusing to goalie after you tried and failed to guard after they afk in the enemy's half is a drag.
In general, I think it's questionable how lax the game's systems are on toxic teammates. They leave - you lose mmr. They spam - you have to either endure it or mute game sound (Spam protection is a joke). You report them - you get queued up with them again.

Other games have shown at least some success with implementing behavior scores, maybe they could try that. Just a thought.

I really want this game to improve, I think there's a lot of potential here. Would be happy to change my review if the team prioritizes player experience over brand deals in the future. Not that those are mutually exclusive, of course. I'm not hating on devs trying to make a bag, more power to you.

Why does a dive take 20minutes to stand back up from? When have you ever seen a goalkeeper try to make a save in football from the goal line and end up stumbling out of the box?

Sloclap please just focus on being a FUN arcade game.

I cannot recommend this game. The latest World Cup update introduces severe inconsistencies and culturally insensitive content that makes the game impossible to enjoy.
While the update introduces national teams, the selection logic is highly questionable. Genuine tournament participants like Croatia have been entirely omitted, while the Russian team has been included despite their official suspension from international sports competitions.
Furthermore, the inclusion of the Russian national anthem - forcing players to listen to it within the application - is deeply disrespectful to European players, particularly the Ukrainian gaming community. Given the ongoing, multi-year conflict and the real-world impact on players, this decision is incredibly tone-deaf and alienating.

I've uninstalled the game, requested a refund, and will be submitting a formal report regarding inappropriate content. The developers urgently need to reassess their content choices and respect international standards.

I probably have more hours in this game than 99% of the people who are leaving reviews. A lot, if not all of their complaints are pretty much valid. I'm here to offer an concise Pros & Cons of the game, and you the reader can make an opinion for yourself. I will start with Cons, because I do enjoy the game and wish to end on a lighter note.

+ Cons:
  - 1. Support of Balenciaga and outright throwing brands in our face, in hopes we'll be good little consumers, shut up, and spend our money to look "richer".
  - 2. The servers and code need improvement, they've come a long way from Beta days, but many still experience desync, network issues, or outright crashes.
  - 3. The Playerbase is 95% entitled, nihilistic, selfish monkeys who tunnel vision on the ball and have no concept of the roles established within the game or in Futsal. They will break any semblance of formation to seek a glimmer of glory and will be more likely than not met with overwhelming failure, then blame the goalie for losing a 4v1. Most games you're lucky if you can manage to get 2 people who are willing to return to defense. In this game  a Man to Man Press is the most effective but that requires everyone to understand their roles and stick to their person, along with the patience to wait for the right time to strike. Thats another issue, they have no patience, if you take more than 3 secs to steal the ball, one of your idiot teammates will run over and double team the ball-holder, which always results in a free pass to whoever they just left open.
  - 4. Stamina Management: If you have ever played a game with a Stam System this isn't an issue, but if you haven't, NEVER let your stam hit zero. You're stamina recovers quicker if you keep it above 25%. Too often you'll see noobs full sprint from one end to the other and then complain they don't have enough stam to return to defense. Just play like Messi, walking to where you need to be and be strategic on how and when you sprint.
  - 5. Broken Relationship: Sloclap has destroyed it relationship with its player base in order to establish better relations with its investors. Which is smart business and wouldn't be as big an issue if they put more effort into listening to the players. I have not seen anything recommended from the community be implemented. There were talks early on about them allowing players to make clubs to represent their local cities and we can see with The World Cup event they have the systems available to make it happen, so I hope they follow through and create it because that one thing by itself will revive the game and bring in revenue to make more mechanical improvements.
  - 6. Defensive Stance: It seems every season they re-tune this mechanic and how fast you can enter and exit the stance. To me, it is one of the core mechanics defining high level skirmishes between players. Making it clunky and slow removes the defensive capabilities of "defensive stance". There's also a cool interaction with it that allows you to freestyle dibble by timing the enter and exit of the stance with perfectly timed movement, slowing it almost entirely removed that fun skill-based interaction.

+ Pros: 
  - 1. Hitboxes: I've seen many people complain about this, but I enjoy the reduction in hitbox sizes. This game requires skill and precision. It heavily punishes bad positioning and passes. There are purely visual bugs where the ball will be just outside your hitbox and your model will reach and give up on the ball, but that is not an error on the game, thats an error on the player. Your positioning was off. People were over-reliant on auto-correction features so they reduced the hitboxes. People were only receiving with Push-Pass (Space/A/X), so they made it take 3secs to recover the ball if you use it to receive a high pass, absolutely destroying your tempo. It forces you to play correct or fail, no safety nets.
  - 2. Goalie: They continued to make goalie more fun to play. It used to be too easy to catch shots on goal and when you missed a catch it was likely due to a bug. Now near misses result in deflections that you opponent can capitalize on, so if you don't read the offense and your defense correctly it will result in your team being scored on.
  - 3. Gameplay: Highly enjoyable, if have good people on both teams. If you're looking for realism, go elsewhere. The high intensity, action movie-style gameplay is what sets the game apart from its competitors and what makes it better than them in my opinion. It could use more permanent game modes though, they have 2-3 game modes (such as 1v1s) that rotate in and out, which makes no sense but whatever.
  - 4. Potential: This game has the potential to be greater than Rocket League, it just needs wiser minds looking out for it's best interests, less influence from outside organizations, and more player-centric changes. As the game is, I can find things to enjoy, but I have to ignore a lot of โฅโฅโฅโฅโฅโฅโฅโฅ. I pray that will lessen with future updates to the game.

+ Scores:
  - Store: 0/10
  - Battlepass: 8/10
  - Character Customization: 7.5/10
  - Technical Stability: 8.5/10
  - Gameplay: 9/10
  - Overall Enjoyment: 8.5/10

this game... this game has changed my life i have made so many good friends and met genuinely nice people and thats a crazy thing to say as a kid >13 the game may have its issues and what not but i still do love this game with all my heart not just for the gameplay but for the rare times that you find people like diamonds in the rough and i have had so many good moments with those people im approaching the 1 year mark of playing this game and i have been so out ragiously happy with the purchase of this game and its crazy to think i couldve never made this purchase i would be a much different person this game has changed me and it only took it 1 year to do all of this change it has taught me that some people you may encounter are not very nice or annoying but others are the opposite and thats why you should never stop trying to reach out to people and its also taught me you dont always have to be the most serious person ever you can cut up and have fun and do stupid things because in the end its not gonna matter this is all just a game one day this game with probably be taken down but the people you had those expeirences with... they are real... they will not be taken down... so enjoy the time you spend with the people you meet dont always be so hateful try being kind and who knows maybe youll meet someone maybe a friend that youll spend your time playing rematch with or maybe other games and i really could go on and on about how great this game is for meeting people for its not all the game that makes me love it so much but the people but i must end this review i hope some finds this helpful (a little tip not everyone is nice as i had said earlier but dont let those people get in your head0 alrigt peace to the one person that read through this all >:P

Game has had a rocky history and is by no means perfect. But I argue that its still one of the best sports games on the market and probably the best soccer game ever. Sloclap are still giving the game support and each balance update brings the game closer to that perfect high intensity soccer that keeps me coming back. Very few games have given me a rush similar to a well executed pass play that ends a 3 min overtime match, but be warned. Solo que can be a coin flip at best, my recommendation is to grab at least one friend before hopping into the ranked modes.

Leaving a negative review, because Sloclap is refusing to take a look into a wrongful anti-cheat ban of one of my friends. Steam has refused a refund (which is expected, he is long past his 2hrs of playtime), so this leaves us with voicing this injustice through a negative review.

If anyone from Sloclap reads this, PLEASE TAKE A LOOK into https://steamcommunity.com/id/cyber95's ban.

We actually enjoy the game, would love to see it succeed, but so far the disgusting lack of accountability and silent refusal to do anything, even just review the ban, has left us with a sour taste in our mouth.

Juego muy divertido, mecanicamente es muy entretenido de aprender y hace que los partidos sean dinamicos y siempre distintos del anterior. Es muy bueno si te gusta el futbol de fantasรญa (Blue lock, inazuma, capitรกn tsubasa) porque no es el tipico juego de futbol, podes disfrutar de cosas no tan realistas y curiosas. Muy bueno para jugar con amigos y divertirte en partidos cortos.

La parte mala del juego: La gente, es un juego donde dependes un 100% de la gente que te toque en tu equipo (A no ser que juegues con amigos en un equipo completo), pero si buscas partida con gente random es tirar una moneda y que te toque alguien muy bueno y que sabe jugar en equipo, como que te toque alguien que no da pases, trollea y le da igual todo (No solo en partidos casuales, tambiรฉn en clasificatorias). Fuera de todo eso, altamente recomendable y denle una oportunidad

I WANT TO LIKE THIS GAME.
But I can't.
Buggy mess, servers are STILL bad to this day, almost every single time I queue ranked, it's the same thing, I either get actual players in my elo, or just people who play the game 24/7 and tryhard/racists. This really feels like Valorant all over again.
People exploit a lot, and this whole faking really just isn't fun. The way the keeper grabs the ball out of THIN AIR is still insane to me. Will they fix the game? Probably not. Will I still play like a slave? From time to time.

reviews.review:
Iโm confused by the country flags currently displayed in the Nations Cup banner on the main menu.

The feature itself is tied to the FIFA World Cup, and from what has been communicated so far, players will only be able to choose from the 32 nations participating in the tournament when the event goes live.

However, the scrolling banner already shows a selection of national flags, including the Russian flag. Russia is not participating in the World Cup, so its inclusion feels inconsistent with the stated theme of the event.

If the intention is to showcase World Cup participants, then only nations that actually qualified should be represented. If the banner is meant to represent countries more broadly, then the current selection criteria are unclear.

Please clarify the logic behind the displayed flags and ensure that the Nations Cup presentation remains consistent with the countries participating in the tournament.

Additionally, given Russiaโs ongoing war against Ukraine and its exclusion from many international sporting competitions, the decision to prominently display the Russian flag in a World Cup-themed event is particularly questionable. 

It is also important to remember that Russia is widely recognized by many governments and international institutions as a state responsible for aggression, war crimes, and attacks against civilians in Ukraine. Given this context, Russia representation is particularly concerning.

I enjoy this game a lot when it comes to the shots and passing but my biggest issue is goalkeeping and dribbling. The dribbles are either move left cut right or using the defensive stance dribbles which are very boring and anyone at a decent rank can just defend you but back in season zero we had techs like ippy slide which was a bug but if they reworked it and made it a real mechanic it would be cool or like if they had a nutmeg feature I think there needs to be more skill moves. If someone does a dive to knock the ball over me. Me and my friend will dive at it sometimes I just dive at the floor or just a bit out of reach but not enough to tip it, I think the jumps for goalkeepers could be reworked a bit.

4th season not even out yet and people already either using exploits or just blatant cheating, using something as 2 goal keepers one on the mid field that can grab balls and just run with it into your own goal. Also balancing is off, if u're mvp of the entire match you should lose less rep then everyone else when u lose.

After Spening 100 hours on the game. I've came to the conclusion that all though this game tries its hardest to be great, it is ruined by the vast amount of exploits and bugs in the game that people can use. The competitive of good plays is gone, when someone can spam a exploited bug for as long as they can unitl it is patched. It leaves more room for people to solo play than to team play due to this, its no longer about making it the best play for the team. But how someone can abuse the exploits to the point they are untouchable.

I've rarely seen a game with gameplay as fundamentally brilliant as Rematch that is held back so hard by everything surrounding it.

The core gameplay is phenomenal. When both teams understand the game and play as a team, Rematch is genuinely one of the most fun competitive multiplayer experiences I've ever had. Unfortunately, getting to those matches is the real challenge.

The biggest issue is the matchmaking system. The purpose of ranked modes isn't just climbing a ladder - it's playing against and alongside people of a similar skill level so you can improve. In fighting games, new players are often encouraged to jump straight into ranked because matchmaking gives them opponents they can actually learn from and have fun while playing since they are not completely outmatched and feel they stand a chance. In Rematch? Forget it.

Queue solo and you'll often end up with a duo and three random players against a full five-stack. Rank differences can be all over the place, and as the player base continues to shrink, it's not uncommon to play against the same people multiple games in a row. What makes this especially bizarre is that Rematch is a purely PvP game. There is no PvE content to fall back on. How a PvP-focused game launched with matchmaking in this state is beyond me.

The community experience doesn't help either. The game does a terrible job teaching even the most basic football concepts. There's no meaningful mandatory tutorial, no role system, and everyone wants to be the main character channeling their inner Messi. If you actually want to win and climb, be prepared to spend a lot of time covering defense while your teammates chase highlights.

And if you're playing goalkeeper? Good luck. Half the time your four teammates are standing next to the opponent's goal waiting for a pass while you're left alone defending against multiple attackers every time possession changes.

Then there's the netcode and ball interactions. The ball isn't driven by fully simulated physics, and many contested situations feel completely random. Two players challenge the ball at the same time? The rebound can go anywhere. Goalkeepers often lose challenges they look like they should win. Sometimes the ball goes straight through a keeper's hands despite them making the correct save attempt. Some of these issues have improved since launch, but they're still noticeable.

The saddest part is that the game's decline isn't exactly surprising. Going from a peak of around 91,000 players to roughly 4,000 concurrent players says a lot about how the community feels about the game's current state and that's what makes this review frustrating to write.

When you have a premade group (or simply get lucky and find teammates who understand football) Rematch is incredible. The core gameplay really is that good.

I wanted to love this game but as someone who mostly plays solo, I can't recommend it.

reviews.review: No, I don't recommend this video game. At the beginning, you could forgive its flaws or even say 'I wish it had this or that,' but the developers don't listen to the community. They only release โฅโฅโฅโฅโฅโฅ updates to add cosmetics for you to waste your money on and little else. They've abandoned it. It's not even fun with friends. You have absolutely no control over the game; I'm more scared of seeing a cross coming in than talking to a woman.

This game is an absolute sluggish nightmare, completely ruined by a pathetic stamina system that makes playing it feel like wading through wet cement. The player AI is completely braindeadโtheir movements are violently erratic and actively sabotage any shred of enjoyable gameplay.

And don't even get me started on the trash-tier goalkeepers; the system is an absolute disgrace. It is utterly broken and unforgivably unplayable for a game that wants to be taken seriously.

In short, this pathetic excuse for a title promised to breathe fresh air into the football genre, but it completely falls flat on its face. It is nothing more than a soul-sucking, mind-numbingly tedious, and relentlessly infuriating waste of time.

As of the time of this review, the recent reviews for this game are mixed. In the game's current state, that's about how varied your experience is going to be too. My review is from the perspective of someone who plays indoor soccer consistently with several years of experience playing IRL. In-game, The highest rank I've reached is only Platinum 1 I believe, so take that how you will. I'll start with a broad and general review of how I feel about the game, then give a few itemized positives and negatives.

I โฅโฅโฅโฅโฅโฅโฅ love this game. This is the type of football/soccer game I've always wanted. You can play in 3v3s, 4v4s, or 5v5s, and there is also a 1v1 duel and "fireball" custom modes that have been introduced so far. Rematch does a fantastic job of replicating the essence of an indoor game. The problems lie in some of the mechanics, gameplay, and "glitching" behavior that players take advantage of, as well as an "in-progress" matchmaking and ranking system that can unfairly pit you against players that are much more experienced and have better technique than you (or match you with players that are significantly lower skilled in the same regard). But in my personal experience, I still have more positive and "normal" experiences with the game (free from weird glitches and server/sync issues or skill mismatches) than I do negative ones. That may be because I usually play with a low ping and a good PC. For a $30 game, I have definitely gotten my money's worth out of it.

Some Positives:
- The potential for this game is huge. The game was built around controlling a single player, and the third person view feels much more immersive than in games like FIFA where the "solo player" game modes where you still have an overhead view of the entire field, and you still have AI teammates. With 10 real players in the game (at least for 5v5s), everyone has to work cohesively as a unit. Things like positioning, communication, passing, making runs, defending, time management... All of the things you have to be aware of and do in the real game, you also have to be aware of and do in this game. Again, as someone that plays indoor consistently IRL, this is awesome. It feels like I'm mentally practicing my skills (especially in terms of positioning and ball movement) when I play this game because other than the physical talent/ability, I can apply the exact same concepts and ideas in my Rematch games to my real games and vice versa.
- The customization is fun and adds a bit of personality to the game, with room for development. While some may dislike or hate the customization options and the current offerings in the battle passes, the fact that it's there and offers players a way to add a personal touch that others can see/appreciate is better than having all the players look more generic.
- Despite what a lot of the haters and negative reviews say about the controls... Once you understand how to use the different kicks/taps/shots/dashes/sprints/etc. effectively and know how ball priority works in the game, most of the time the controls work and respond as I would expect them to (in my experience). It wasn't always the case in the past. I remember there was a bug where sometimes if you gave the correct input for a standing tackle but you were sprinting just before that, it would still make you slide tackle instead. But that issue and others have been addressed and fixed, leading to my next point.
- The developers DO listen and are making definite progress. This is probably a hot take. But I feel like the negative reviewers and the haters that complain about Sloclap not making the right decisions or not fixing anything are from people that don't understand game development and/or soccer. There are objectively bad things about the game, 100%. If there weren't, the reviews wouldn't be mixed. Go back about a year from this post and read some of the negative reviews and you'll see a lot of the same kinds of complaints about glitches and gameplay and all sorts of problems. Then, go read some of the developer's patch notes or public forum posts (They usually post a blog/update on the r/Rematch subreddit online). They are listening and they are addressing each problem that players complain about or experience. They have clearly stated their intentions for getting rid of "glitchy" movements that are unpredictable, and they have delivered on that promise on multiple occasions. They can't just fix everything overnight though. A lot of people aren't pleased with the progress from Season 0 to now, but I started in the middle of Season 0 and am happy with the communication from Sloclap and am optimistic about what this game could become.

Some Negatives:
- The player population right now is very low. A lot of the community is in the "Dead Game" doom and gloom stage. And it sometimes feels that way. If you want to play a game during off hours of the day (like at noon on a Wednesday or something), matchmaking is going to take a long time, and if you find a match it might not be with players of the same relative skill level. I only play in the US West servers so I can only imagine what it's like on other servers.
- The current iteration of the game is based off of a client-side sync instead of a server-side sync. I don't have any insight as to why the initial decision to NOT sync on the server-side in the first place, but I imagine it had something to do with cost and was ultimately a business decision. Regardless, the effects of this can be seen in games with players that have higher pings. You'll notice more glitchy behavior with the ball, instances of "teleporting" and such. There is a mechanic around ball priority in this game, and high latency and ping definitely affects what players see versus what players with lower ping see. Sloclap has stated that they intend to improve their servers and switch to a server-side sync soon in the future, so hopefully that will make things better.
- Toxic players and the reporting system. Toxic players are in every multiplayer game. History has proven that anonymity and the internet is a combination that can result in the worst types of behavior emerging from the human psyche. The type of toxicity you'll see in this game generally takes on the form of a selfish type of player who does one or more of the following: Never passes, never plays defense, spams "Good Job!" ironically and aggressively when someone makes a mistake or just doesn't pass to them (like in Rocket League - "What a Save!"), will try and steal the ball from you even though you're on the same team, will use voice chat to just be a toxic piece of โฅโฅโฅโฅ, will leave the goal wide open when it's their turn to play keeper... etc. And unfortunately, the current report system needs some work. With a low population and a non-reliable reporting system, it's possible to have some days where it's just not worth it to play the game.
 - The matchmaking and ranking system needs work. Right now, matchmaking does not favor new players, or anyone that's really below Platinum or sometimes even Diamond. I think this is partially due to the mentioned low player count and not having enough people of equivalent skill level to match with sometimes, but progressing through the ranks can be random and based off of luck sometimes. For example, I might play amazingly for all of my games, make the right passes, get a goal or two, make great saves as keeper, basically do everything I possibly can for my team, and then we might still lose because of a mismatch in skill with the rest of my team, or against the other team. And that might happen 5 games in a row. Add onto that the possibility of running into new or repeat toxic players and it can get frustrating.


Ultimately I know there's reason to hate it. But for what the game is, how far it's come, and where it's going, I love it and happily recommend it with the caveat that it is still a work in progress and your experiences may vary.

I do not recommend REMATCH.

This game is an absolute mess in its current state. The goalkeeper system is terrible, inconsistent, and often feels completely broken. Too many matches are decided by frustrating mechanics instead of actual skill.

The biggest issue is the ridiculous abuse of unrealistic dribbling mechanics. Match after match, players spam the same absurd moves and combinations that look more like exploits than football. At times it genuinely feels like people are using macros to chain together nonsense dribbles that completely break the flow of the game.

Instead of rewarding positioning, teamwork, passing, and football IQ, the game rewards whoever can abuse the most broken mechanics. The result is a frustrating, repetitive, and often miserable experience that barely resembles football.

What could have been a great football game has turned into a showcase of poor balance, questionable design choices, and gimmicky gameplay. Until the developers address the goalkeeper issues and the abuse of these unrealistic mechanics, I cannot recommend this game to anyone.
good game
bodimais

Honestly, I don't know whether I should recommend Rematch in its current state or not. That's why I think Steam should have a middle option between recommending and not recommending a game.

Let's start with the positives. I think it's a fun game to play with friends, it has a pretty good concept, a nice visual style, and overall it's a genuinely entertaining and enjoyable experience. And thankfully, they've improved the server issues that were especially noticeable in Latin America.

Now let's talk about the negatives.

First and foremost, the community is awful. It's terrible. The people playing can be incredibly toxic, and I'm constantly running into kids with completely blown-out microphones screaming insults like monkeys and demonstrating a remarkable lack of brain cells. They're selfish with the ball, play terribly, start trolling, deliberately sabotage their own team, and generally don't play the game the way it should be played. At least, that's been my experience.

There also aren't as many people playing anymore compared to before. Sometimes when you queue up for a match, there simply aren't enough players available. And the reason is pretty obvious.

The hitboxes are quite bad, some of the physics need improvement, but the biggest issue right now is that the hitbox system feels completely broken and desperately needs work. There are several other aspects of the game that should be improved as well, and considering that the game costs around $15 (or at least that's the price here in Argentina) I honestly think that's a bit ridiculous.

Among many other issues that have already been pointed out by the rest of the community, that's essentially where the game stands right now. It currently has Mixed Reviews worldwide, and honestly, that's completely understandable.

So here's my final verdict:

The game has a lot of potential, and unfortunately the developers aren't taking advantage of it or pushing it as much as they should. If they don't make significant improvements, the game will probably die.

That said, I'm still giving it a positive review because of the good moments I had playing with my friends, the laughs we shared, those goods moments when we won matches, and the fact that I've put quite a few hours into it. And I have to admit that, despite its major flaws and shortcomings, it's still a fun and entertaining game to play.

New brand collabs! The game was very promising at the start. Good bones, a great premise to mixup the football game formula, and an interesting art style. The developers previous games were pretty solid as well, giving a confidence in this project. Sadly, after nearly a year of time given and multiple "gameplay seasons", the game is still in a sad state that has failed to live up to expectations.

TLDR: Good game (especially with friends), abysmal player base. I'll still play this garbage though.

I don't exactly take this game too seriously due to the number of bugs and inconsistencies so I try to just have fun with the jank with pals. HOWEVER, the experience is heavily marred by a significant portion of the player base. A good half of the games I've experience have had some really rude and sore people that just refuse to learn and actively sabotage their teams. A real "one rotten egg spoils the bunch" scenario on both sides. For a paid online-only multiplayer game, you'd think this kind of stuff would be more rare, especially with a smaller, supposedly dedicated community. I don't know what it is about (loosely) competitive sports games that attracts the worst kinds of people, but they paid money to kick a ball, who am I to judge if they quit mid match if they don't get to play the way they want.

Edit: Almost forgot to mention the outdated battlepass model, fomo is a terrible business practice and my battlepass should never be taken from me after I paid for it. Get with the times SloClap! The quality of cosmetics and BP items are also very lackluster. Very few gems, and even those don't feel worthwhile. Just be content with the freebies you get for this paid, online only game.

First. the recent Mixed Reviews the time im writing it its 68% positive.
now about the game and my opinion-perspective.

pros
1 the gameplay is unique and fun.
2 easy to learn, hard to master.
2.1 you have to learn how to shoot-attack, how to defend, how to goal keeping, or you can master 1 be ok in the other 2
3 Goalkeeper is Fun and vital role.
4 graphics are beautyfull
5 the "skins"-edit they got for the character-the field, are plenty and you can make your character never look identical with others as a complete set (the whole set= uniform-face-hairs-facial)
6 Colaborations with famous players, like Ronaldihno
7 read their roadtrip. They going to make Big changes till the end of 2026 according to their post.
8 the price for the replayability is ok, but i would preffer it cheaper or even free (check 3 in cons)

Cons.
1= desync lag issues. Its not gamebreaking while a friend of mine who just bought it with  140ping is having fun and not seein big issues at least in low elo games.
2= the passes, oh this can be frustrating, while i managed to control somehow, its still hard to use them and generaly bad design imo.
3= most people are complaining about microtransactions, while the game costs a good ammount of money.
BUT they are still skins, not p2w. i Put it in the cons because its already 25e the base game, and you have to pay extra for not that cheap skins and season passes.

P.S
thats my opinion, and keep in mind that im Fun of SIFU, but i tried to be as objective as possible.

P.S2 the friend of mine who bought the game, has below minimal specs, and runs smoothly 45-60fps in low graphics.

Even though the game is fun and I will keep playing it, the game is not worth the price. There is simply a lack of content, and what I think is the worst of all, there is a battle pass (here it's called the Captain Pass) in the game which blocks out most of the fun customisation items. If the game were free, I would get it that there is a game pass, but I paid 25 clams for this game, and now the game wants me to pay even more to get the cool items. There are also 3 types of currency (for some reason) tokens, blocks and quants. To get items from the free rewards, you pay with these tokens, but often you also have to pay for the better items from the captain pass to be able to progress in the free pass EVEN THOUGH you do NOT get the items from the captain pass unless you pay for the captain pass. Then there are blocks and quants. Quants are the V-bucks of this game, and you can buy everything with them. Blocks are just the lesser version of quants, and you can buy a few items with them. The blocks are fine as a currency, but the quants are just horrible. You need 1k quants to get the captain's pass, and if you do not pay for the captain's pass, you only get 300 quants in 1 season. Again, if the game were free, I would get it, but I paid 25 bucks for this game, and now if I wanted to obtain the better items, I would need to pay more money for the quant.

Also, the game can be very laggy sometimes. 

Overall the game is fun but absolutely not worth 25 bucks.

Disappointed how this game turned out. Mechanics are cheesy and extremely arcade like. Bugs are common leaving for some frustrating losses or plays. I wanted a more authentic futbol game with this beautiful art style but its just rocket league with humans instead of cars. There's definitely room to enjoy it but honestly I'd say it's because it lacks competition. I play time to time but would never commit in its current state. Fifa Street did this better back in 2012. 

Other than that the game lacks content. You'll always have more currency than you need. Apart from the customization there's really nothing to earn other than a rank. Just like any team based game; don't bother if you don't have friends to play with. There's 3v3, 4v4 and 5v5 but that's it. 4v4 isn't even popular and 3's is boring. Give it a shot if you got money to spare or really like futbol games. You can skip this.

reviews.review:
Rematch is a really interesting game that stands out for trying something a bit different. It has a strong core idea and some genuinely fun moments that make it worth playing, even if it doesnโt fully reach its potential.

The gameplay is solid and enjoyable when everything clicks. Matches can feel exciting and fast-paced, and thereโs a good foundation here that shows what the game could become with more refinement.

Where Rematch struggles is in how inconsistent it can feel at times. Some systems feel a bit underdeveloped, and it seems like the game doesnโt fully take advantage of all the ideas it introduces. Thatโs where the โwasted potentialโ feeling comes in.

Even with those issues, thereโs still a lot to like. The game has personality and a fun loop that keeps it engaging for a while, especially when youโre in the middle of a good match.

Overall, Rematch is a decent game with a lot of potential that isnโt fully realized yet. Itโs still enjoyable, but it feels like it could have been something much bigger with more polish and content.

Porรฉm, o mais chato que achei atรฉ agora foram os players. Posso afirmar com toda certeza que 80% deles nunca jogou uma partida de futebol e ainda tem sรญndrome de protagonista. Todo mundo quer ser o autor do gol e artilheiro; ninguรฉm se contenta em ser zagueiro, goleiro ou volante. ร bem comum ver 3 jogadores saindo das suas posiรงรตes e deixando de marcar o adversรกrio para tentar tomar a bola de um รบnico player.

Au niveau technique, pas mal de bug, des gardiens qui arrรชtent les buts dans les cages normal, des animations prioritaires sur d'autres mรชme si vous avez 2 m d'avance... La camรฉra est trรจs pรฉnible, impossible de dรฉzoomer davantage, ce qui est idiot lorsqu'on essaie de construire une action. Bref, quelques parties agrรฉables mais 2% environ sur la totalitรฉ des matchs faits donc non merci ca ira bien.

I really want to love this game, 

At launch it had so much potential, but a year+ later they really haven't resolved any of the systems that people abuse. Ranked is such a โฅโฅโฅโฅ show because it will match you with anyone still it seems, and more often than not it seems to love the 5 solo vs/ the 5 stack. 

No penalties for folks leaving so ranked matches fall apart quick, the moment 1 person leaves it snowballs hard. 

When this game is good it is GREAT, but those moments are getting further and further apart. There are no dedicated roles which seemed like an interesting idea at launch, however it just leads to Keepers bailing if they don't want that role, and in the best scenarios leads to a mad dash to cover who bailed on their position. 

Beyond the community issues, desync's, and latency issues are frequent. Ball prioritization appears to be entirely broken you'll watch Keepers lay down to glove a ball and it'll pass through them, people can juggle basically forever if you/they have the lower ping. 

this game could've been great, I put in the hours to try but almost all of it was chasing a fleeting moment of awesome the arcade dynamic was an interesting mix up but the Dev's just failed to implement it properly and reinforced the wrong behaviors.

This game is a catastrophic dumpster fire disguised as a competitive sports game. About 60% of the time itโs genuinely fun, and the other 40% feels like the developers personally broke into my house and slammed my head into my desk. The amount of rage caused by the glitches, bugs, garbage hit registration, and the unbelievably insufferable community is beyond comprehension. I have seen less toxicity in nuclear waste sites.

If youโre thinking about playing this game solo, donโt. Save yourself while you still can. Without a semi-competent squad of friends, this game is simply a soccer game where everyone is a cry baby and clinically incapable of passing the ball. I don't think iv'e played more than three matches in a row without some loser kicking it purposely into their own net. Every match is either the greatest experience ever created or a brutal reminder that human intelligence has its limits.

To put it simply: you never know if you lost because the other team was better or because the game itself decided it hated you personally.

After 30 hours, I can confidently say no other game has brought me this close to uninstalling, reinstalling, and uninstalling again within the same hour. This game somehow delivers the highest highs and the most suffering imaginable. Itโs like being trapped in an abusive relationship with a soccer ball.

Overall Rating: 4/10

The gameplay, as in "kicking the ball" is OK. I like that it's about actual teamwork rather than pseudo-teamwork like "healer follows tank, press E to revive". Aiming shots is much harder on controller than on MKB, I think.

The game needs a behavior/teamwork score system. People spawn as goalkeeper and immediately leave the goal because it's too boring for them. Others dribble thinking they're prime Messi and lose posession instead of just passing. I pass all the time and play defense a lot - I'd say I'm a team player and I want to play with other team players more often. Let the lone wolves play against themselves.

Sometimes people leave at the start of the game and you're forced to play 2v3. The game doesn't do anything to alleviate it. It will even punish you if you leave (in Rocket League you can leave for free). The post-game screen is SO LONG GOD. Just give me 3 seconds to team up and then let me go. Maybe don't force me to watch the goal celebration and replay for the last goal if I lost 0-4?

The game "feels" like a free to play game. It has:
- daily and weekly quests to keep you addicted
- premium in-game currency
- horrible in-game marketing for sports brands. I hated it in Rocket League, maybe you could stand out by NOT doing that?
- grindy cosmetics unlock system, god forbid you just buy the cosmetic you want for the non-premium in-game currency

The art direction is bland or "no sovl". The characters look like those cartoony ones from generic smut book covers (see "Icebreaker"). The "AR" or stadiums are lifeless. I feel like someone really tried but they're pretty much GIFs put on the walls. To see what I mean, wouldn't it be nice if players had condensation coming out of their mouths in the ice biome stadium? Wouldn't it be "kino" if the captain had a little yellow armband on the player model? 

I used the character creation feature a lot and I wish I could say more but it's not "it". I'd prefer it if the character creation options didn't let you go too crazy. It feels like half the people play max vitiligo slider with afros with 88 on their back - yes I know kids play this game. The "pick you color palette" thing at the start of the match is stupid, just always pick captain's. It's really difficult to make your character not look like a teenager. The "oldness" slider at max setting gives you two wrinkles.

Even ROBLOX couldnโt come close to this level of quality. The game feels polished, responsive, and genuinely enjoyable to play. Matchmaking is solid, matches feel competitive, and the mechanics clearly show attention to detail rather than copying other games. As someone who loves football, I can appreciate how much effort went into the gameplay from ball control and deflections to player positioning and reactions.
The input system actually feels smooth and rewarding once you understand the timing. For example, in situations with high balls near the box, the game consistently rewards proper positioning and decision-making instead of random outcomes. The keeper mechanics are also impressive; defensive stance and save animations feel natural, and close-range saves create tense moments instead of looking awkward or scripted.
Overall, the developers clearly invested time into refining the gameplay experience and building something with real potential. Instead of feeling unfinished, the game already has a strong foundation and can only get better with future updates.

El emparejamiento es horrible, la personalizacion es aceptable. Despues de un par de partidos se vuelve aburrido porque la gente en su mayoria juegan a hacerse los buenos, pierden la pelota y te meten gol.
Muchas veces estas con 3/5 minutos de cola, cuando entras a una partida uno de tu equipo se va afk y te deja en desventaja. A veces te tocan troll, te hacen perder la partida. Lo reportas y en la proxima te toca de vuelta. Y hablando de eso en tu equipo te tocan unos monos que no sabes como fueron capaces de ingresar los datos de la tarjeta para comprar este juego que deberia ser gratis por el poco contenido que tiene y lo mal hechas que estan las animaciones y demas cosas. Ni se molestaron en ponerles voces en las celebraciones de gol, que encima son horribles. 
Los movimientos son toscos y cuando por fin te podes sacar alguna partida tu equipo la caga por hacerse los buenos y perder la pelota. 
lleno de bugs, me paso muchisimas veces perder porque estando atajando el personaje se buguea cuando tendria que atajar la pelota.
Es muy repetitivo, todas las jugadas que hacen son iguales. Pegarles al muro y despues patear o tirar un centro.
Detesto que no tenga un chat en el cual escribir. Se hubieran ahorrado una mala reseรฑa si dejaran recontra bardear en el chat. Pareciera que es una demo

I don't post reviews very often, but I feel like this game gets an unnecessary amount of hate.

This game is developed by the same people behind Absolver, and Sifu, and you can really feel that fighting action esc playstyle in this game.

Although the game does have it's problems (what game doesn't) I feel like people tend to forget that this is Slocap's first multiplayer game, of course there is going to be net code issues (especially in a physics/priority based game)

Actual Issues
- Slow updates (typically each season will change the meta, because they will release the majority of balance patches with the new seasons)
- Feels like they spend a good amount of time pushing out cosmetics, but not enough time playtesting their own product before releasing patches
- Playerbase is full of main character syndrome egoist who think they can dribble past 3 people (even if you can just pass to the open man, it really ain't that hard)
- Goalkeeper keeps getting nerfed into the ground (They said they want it to be the final boss, yet keep making it harder to actually save goals)
- Dibble spam, now this is something they keep trying to tweak, and I really think there needs to be a balance here, even in real soccer, there are time you have to dribble past 1/2 people to open up a passing lane, this is no different, the problem comes when you can dribble for literally 30 seconds without slowing down. (This is something they are addressing in the next patch)
- Completing the battle pass can be difficult with the new system they implemented

Now onto why I love this game
- I have never played a game that encapsulates that competitive feeling you get from playing sports as well as Rematch does
- Genuinely satisfying animations that feel fluid and chain together really well
- Really fun to do build up plays with teammates (If your teammates aren't egoists)
- Really fun to 1v1 people and use your skill to get past them on the field (As much as people hate it, you should be able to dribble past people in a 1v1 if you are more skilled)
- Goalkeeper is actually enjoyable, so you don't feel annoyed when you get rotated in the box.

I think this game is worth every penny, and it reminds me of the early days of Rocket League, they have a long way to go before this game is even close to being considered polished. But I really think it is worth the try, and as long as you have some patience with the dev team (and especially the playerbase) it is an amazing game.
